zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrig於1448年出生時，他的父親Sir William Douglas 3rd Baron of Drumlanrig33歲，母親Margaret Carlyle38歲。他於1470年11月5日與Janet Scott在蘇格蘭澤爾吉爾可爾布可洛結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1498年在英國蘇格蘭度福利爾德盧姆藍利 卡素享年50歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrig于1448年出生时，他的父亲Sir William Douglas 3rd Baron of Drumlanrig33岁，母亲Margaret Carlyle38岁。他于1470年11月5日与Janet Scott在苏格兰澤爾吉爾可爾布可洛结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1498年在英国苏格兰度福利爾德盧姆藍利 卡素享年50岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrig was born in 1448, his father, Sir William Douglas 3rd Baron of Drumlanrig, was 33 and his mother, Margaret Carlyle, was 38. He married Janet Scott on 5 November 1470, in Buccleuch, Selkirkshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1498, in Drumlanrig Castle, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 50, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrig est né en 1448, son père, Sir William Douglas 3rd Baron of Drumlanrig, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Margaret Carlyle, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Janet Scott le 5 novembre 1470, à Buccleuch, Selkirkshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1498, à Drumlanrig Castle, Dumfriesshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrig 1448 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William Douglas 3rd Baron of Drumlanrig 33 und seine Mutter Margaret Carlyle 38. Er hat am 5. Er starb 1498 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Drumlanrig Castle, Dumfriesshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Scotland begraben.

Lui sposato Janet Scott il 5 novembre 1470, nel Buccleuch, Selkirkshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrigが1448年に生まれた時，父Sir William Douglas 3rd Baron of Drumlanrigは33歳，母Margaret Carlyleは38歳であった。1470年11月5日にスコットランドセルキルクシュアイアブククレウチでJanet Scottと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1498年にイギリススコットランドダムフリーシュアイアドルムランリグ カストルで50歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrig(이)가 1448년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Douglas 3rd Baron of Drumlanrig(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Carlyle(은)는 38세였다. 그는 1470년 11월 5일 스컷랜드 셀커크실르 버치레우치에서 Janet Scott(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1498년 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리실르 드럼랜리그 카스틀에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Janet Scott em 5 de novembro de 1470, em Buccleuch, Selkirkshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrig родился в 1448 г., его отцу по имени Sir William Douglas 3rd Baron of Drumlanrig было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Carlyle, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Janet Scott 5 ноября 1470 г. в местности букклеуч, селкиркшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1498 г. в местности друмланриг кастл, думфриессхир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности Шотландия.
